name: Checked vs unchecked exceptions
question_text: Which Java class is the root of unchecked exceptions that the compiler does not force you to declare or catch?
general_feedback: java.lang.RuntimeException and its subclasses (and Error) are unchecked. Subclasses of Exception other than RuntimeException are checked and must be declared or handled.
correct_feedback: Correct — RuntimeException and its subclasses are unchecked.

answers.feedback: RuntimeException and its subclasses are unchecked.
Most Exception subclasses are checked; RuntimeException is the unchecked branch.
IOException is a checked exception — the compiler forces you to declare or catch it.
Throwable is the common ancestor of all exceptions and errors, not the 'unchecked' root specifically.
